name: Muslim Wellness Foundation
mission: Muslim Wellness Foundation (MWF) is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization dedicated to promoting healing and well-being in American Muslim communities through dialogue, education and training. The MWF approach to health is interdisciplinary, intergenerational, spiritually grounded, community based, and justice-oriented. From being the first Muslim organization in the country to offer Mental Health First Aid (MHFA) trainings to establishing the annual Black Muslim Psychology Conference (BMPC), MWF has been an outspoken leader in fostering visions for wellness and healing. Our programs demonstrate our keen awareness of the link between oppression, wellness and how communities heal, grow, thrive, and experience joy in the midst of it all. We will continue to lead the way in shifting the conversation on mental health, addiction and trauma - from one narrowly focused on stigma to a healing-centered model of collective well-being, faith, identity and belonging. Join us on this journey.
theoryOfChange: The MWF approach to health is interdisciplinary, intergenerational, spiritually grounded, community based, and justice-oriented, aiming to shift from stigma-focused models to healing-centered models of collective well-being.

strengths_deep_dive: The Muslim Wellness Foundation's operational model is characterized by extreme financial leanness. By maintaining a <cite id="1">100.0% program expense ratio</cite>, the organization ensures that donor contributions are not diluted by administrative overhead. This efficiency is further evidenced by its <cite id="1">fundraising cost of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, suggesting a reliance on organic growth and volunteer-led initiatives. Furthermore, the organization has shown rapid financial expansion, with <cite id="2">revenue growing from $99,778 in 2021 to $223,205 in 2023</cite>, a <cite id="2">CAGR of 49.6%</cite>. This growth supports its specialized curriculum, including <cite id="3">Dialogue, education, and training</cite> modules designed for the unique psychological landscape of American Muslims.
rich_narrative.summary: The Muslim Wellness Foundation (MWF) is a specialized nonprofit organization dedicated to promoting healing and emotional well-being within the American Muslim community through education and advocacy. Operating primarily in the United States, MWF addresses the intersection of culture, religion, and mental health, focusing on health equity and community empowerment. In the 2023 fiscal year, the organization reported <cite id="2">total revenue of $223,205</cite>, representing a significant <cite id="2">3-year revenue CAGR of 49.6%</cite>. Despite its relatively small scale, the foundation maintains a <cite id="1">100.0% program expense ratio</cite>, ensuring that all reported spending is directed toward its core mission. Key initiatives include <cite id="3">Mental Health First Aid (MHFA) trainings</cite> and the <cite id="4">Black Muslim Psychology Conference (BMPC)</cite>, which provide critical spaces for dialogue and professional development. The organization operates with <cite id="1">high fundraising efficiency</cite>, reporting a cost of $0.00 per $1 raised, though it faces challenges regarding financial liquidity and limited third-party data verification.
